dataset: BMW27.blend (official Blender benchmark demo scene from download.blender.org/demo/test/)
case_id: carShell mesh object (renamed to CarBody by setup_task.sh)

pi_items.source: Live testing: file exists at this path, size 3,148,419 bytes. Downloaded from official Blender demo archive at download.blender.org/demo/test/BMW27.blend.zip by the environment's setup_blender.sh script.

Design choice by task creator. Live testing confirmed /home/ga/BlenderProjects/ directory exists and is writable by user 'ga'. The setup_task.sh script cleans this file before each run. The export_result.sh script checks this path. Blender 4.2.4 saves with zstd compression (magic bytes 28b52ffd), which the export script handles correctly.

Design choice by task creator. Live testing confirmed rendering works: a test render at 4 samples, 25% resolution produced a 282,001-byte PNG. The export_result.sh script checks this path using PIL to get dimensions and format.

Live testing confirmed: setup_task.sh renames the 'carShell' mesh object to 'CarBody'. The initial_state.json output from a live run showed car_body_object='CarBody', car_body_original_name='carShell', car_body_vertex_count=1765. The object is visible, selectable, and has the CarBodyMaterial applied. NOTE: carShell (1765 verts) is NOT the largest mesh in BMW27.blend — TireRubber has 8957 verts. The setup script identifies carShell by name, not by vertex count.

Live testing: a minimal test render (4 samples, 25% resolution = 480x270) produced 282KB. At the task's actual settings (32 samples, 50% resolution = 960x540) with a material applied, renders would be 500KB+. The 50KB threshold is very conservative and appropriate.

Live testing in Blender 4.2.4: successfully created node via tree.nodes.new('ShaderNodeTexNoise'). Node exists and has Scale input (default 5.0), Detail input (default 2.0), Roughness input (default 0.5). Confirmed via official Blender Python API docs at docs.blender.org/api/current/bpy.types.ShaderNodeTexNoise.html.

Live testing in Blender 4.2.4: successfully created node via tree.nodes.new('ShaderNodeValToRGB'). This is the internal name for the 'Color Ramp' node. Default has 2 stops: position 0.0 (black) and position 1.0 (white).

Live testing: already present in the starting material (CarBodyMaterial). The setup creates it with Base Color (0.4, 0.4, 0.4, 1.0), Metallic 0.0, Roughness 0.5. The agent needs to create a second one and modify both.
Live testing in Blender 4.2.4: successfully created via tree.nodes.new('ShaderNodeMixShader'). Has Fac input (factor) and two Shader inputs.

Live testing in Blender 4.2.4: successfully created via tree.nodes.new('ShaderNodeTexVoronoi'). Node name in UI is 'Voronoi Texture'. Inputs: ['Vector', 'W', 'Scale', 'Detail', 'Roughness', 'Lacunarity', 'Smoothness', 'Exponent', 'Randomness']. Outputs: ['Distance', 'Color', 'Position', 'W', 'Radius']. Default Scale is 5.0. NOTE: ShaderNodeTexMusgrave was removed in Blender 4.1+; Voronoi is the correct replacement for surface detail.

Live testing in Blender 4.2.4: successfully created via tree.nodes.new('ShaderNodeBump'). Has Strength input (default 1.0), Distance input, Height input, and Normal output. The task specifies setting Strength to 0.3.

Task description specifies Scale 5.0 for the Noise Texture. This matches the Blender default for ShaderNodeTexNoise Scale input. Confirmed via Blender Python API documentation. The value is a design choice — the agent must set this value.

Task description specifies Detail 8.0 for the Noise Texture. Blender default is 2.0, so the agent MUST change this from default. This is a design choice — 8.0 produces finer noise pattern suitable for realistic rust distribution. The export_result.sh correctly reads this value via n.inputs['Detail'].default_value.

Task description specifies Scale 15.0 for the Voronoi Texture. Blender default for Voronoi Scale is 5.0, so the agent must change this. 15.0 produces fine cell-like corrosion pitting. The export_result.sh reads this via n.inputs['Scale'].default_value.

Task description specifies Strength 0.3 for the Bump node. Blender default is 1.0, so the agent must change this. 0.3 gives subtle surface perturbation appropriate for corrosion. The export_result.sh reads this via n.inputs['Strength'].default_value.

Task description specifies a Color Ramp with black-to-white threshold at position 0.4. This means approximately 40% of the surface will show rust and 60% clean metal. Default Color Ramp has stops at 0.0 (black) and 1.0 (white); the agent must move the white stop to position 0.4 or add a stop there. The export_result.sh reads all color_ramp.elements positions and colors.

privileged_info_summary: Target object is 'CarBody', which is the car body shell mesh originally named 'carShell' in the official BMW27.blend Blender benchmark scene (1765 vertices, 1567 faces). The starting material is 'CarBodyMaterial' — a plain grey Principled BSDF with Base Color (0.4, 0.4, 0.4), Metallic 0.0, Roughness 0.5, connected to a Material Output node (2 nodes, 1 link total). The agent must build a procedural rust-over-metal shader node graph with exactly these nodes: (1) Noise Texture with Scale=5.0 and Detail=8.0 (default Detail is 2.0, so it must be changed), (2) Color Ramp with a threshold stop at position 0.4 to create a sharp rust mask (default stops are at 0.0 and 1.0), (3) two Principled BSDFs — clean metal (dark grey ~#2A2A2A which is sRGB R=0.165/G=0.165/B=0.165, Metallic=1.0, Roughness=0.2) and rust (brown ~#8B4513 'SaddleBrown' which is sRGB R=0.545/G=0.271/B=0.075, Metallic=0.0, Roughness=0.8), (4) Mix Shader driven by Color Ramp output as Factor, blending both BSDFs, output connected to Material Output Surface, (5) Voronoi Texture with Scale=15.0 (default is 5.0, must be changed) connected through a Bump node with Strength=0.3 (default is 1.0, must be changed) to the Normal input of both BSDFs. Render engine is Cycles with 32 samples at 960x540 (1920x1080 at 50%). Expected outputs: /home/ga/BlenderProjects/rust_render.png (PNG, minimum 50KB) and /home/ga/BlenderProjects/rust_bmw.blend. Blender 4.2.4 LTS is installed at /opt/blender/blender. The Blender version uses zstd compression for .blend files (magic bytes 28b52ffd). All node types use standard Blender 4.2.4 internal names: ShaderNodeTexNoise, ShaderNodeValToRGB, ShaderNodeBsdfPrincipled, ShaderNodeMixShader, ShaderNodeTexVoronoi, ShaderNodeBump. Note: ShaderNodeTexMusgrave was removed in Blender 4.1+.
